deck_title: Hoshino Premium Bottled Matcha — Launch Proposal
generated_by: ace-review 2026-07-05 (graph: 4 active judgments, 4 precedents via shared attributes, 1 same-client win)

design.concept: washi + stone-milled matcha: the deck borrows its look from premium tea packaging — unbleached paper, deep green, a thin gold seam
design.signature: vertical kicker labels set like the side of a tea canister, with a single gold rule under each statement

slides.support: Affluent women in their 50s don't buy premium tea to follow wellness fashion — they buy a daily ritual, often shared with a spouse. This exact framing won our last Hoshino pitch.

evidence.why: lead with the audience insight, features after — 3 supporting outcomes, 1 contradiction (see risk slide)
same client, same segment: the ritual story beat two competitors who led with product specs (out-hoshino-2026-02)

slides.statement: TV is where she already is.
slides.support: TV leads; a family-mediated short-form cutdown extends reach. Expert endorsement is this segment's trust lever — the spot carries a tea-master's voice.

evidence.why: owned research on exactly this segment; also backed the winning Hoshino Feb deck
survey-grounded decks: 5 supporting outcomes, zero contradictions — the brain's strongest rule

persona: Household income 8M+ — she curates, she doesn't economize
Keeps the family's health routines; her 3pm tea is the day's quiet reset
Trusts craft and expertise, not influencers
Premium is self-reward — permission she grants herself
slides.support: Deliberately NOT a 'women 40–60 wellness' umbrella. One narrow persona, one habit moment.

evidence.why: broad 'everyone' targets read as generic and lose (supported by out-hoshino-2026-02, out-fittech-2026-04)
'affluent F50 buy premium as self-reward, not thrift' — the insight that won with the identical segment (out-aeonmart-2026-01)

frames.caption: 3pm. Kitchen light. A kettle hums — no voiceover yet.
Two cups set down on the counter. Her husband looks up.
The pour, slowed. The green blooms — the pause IS the product.
Tea-master's voice over the craft close: stone-milled, single-origin. Bottle reveal, gold seam.

slides.support: Premium justified through craft and ritual, never through price. Product facts (origin, umami, zero sugar) arrive only after the story.

evidence.why: ritual-anchored TV creative is this client's proven pattern
story first, specs after — the structure that beat spec-led competitors

slides.statement: Every choice here has a receipt.
bullets: Same-client proof: the Feb 2026 sugar-free tea launch won with this exact ritual framing (pres-hoshino-2026-02-final)
Segment proof: self-reward insight won with identical affluent-F50 audience at AeonMart (pres-aeonmart-2026-01-final)
Data-grounded: every media claim traces to our n=500 F50 survey (data-survey-f50-media)
Pre-mortem done: we checked our three known loss modes before writing a single frame

evidence.why: the provenance chain is the pitch

avoided_risks.lesson: An insight that contradicts the client's own consumer research kills the pitch. Action: validate the ritual/self-reward insight against Hoshino's in-house data BEFORE the presentation.
Broad targeting reads as generic. We locked ONE persona (Keiko, 56) before any creative work.
Lost on price after a mid-competition budget cut — not a content failure, but a real risk. This deck never leads with price, and we scope a digital-cutdown fallback if TV budget wobbles.

prediction.expects: success — same-client ritual framing won in Feb 2026, reinforced by survey-backed (0.95), narrow-persona (0.7) and insight-first (0.6) judgments, all with matching evidence on this exact segment
prediction.risks: if this fails: (1) the ritual insight conflicts with Hoshino's own consumer research (Sakura pattern), or (2) budget cut mid-competition turns it into a price fight (Tokai pattern)
